subject: Science
question_text: Which of the following options correctly shows the flow of plants, animals, and decomposers in a habitat? (1) plants → animals → decomposers (2) decomposers → animals ← plants (3) plants → animals, plants → decomposers, animals → decomposers (4) plants → animals, decomposers (unlinked)
correct_answer: (3) plants → animals, with arrows from both plants and animals pointing to decomposers. Decomposers break down dead remains of both plants and animals.
working: Decomposers break down the dead remains of ALL living things (both plants and animals). So arrows should go from plants to decomposers AND from animals to decomposers. Option (1) only shows a one-way chain (plants → animals → decomposers), which wrongly implies decomposers only break down animals.

subject: Science
question_text:
Study the diagram of a pond below. Which of the following statements is/are true?
A. The floating plants make up one population.
B. There are two populations of animals in the pond.
C. There is one community of plants and one community of animals in the habitat.
(1) B only (2) C only (3) A and B only (4) A, B and C
background_context: The diagram shows a pond with multiple types of floating plants on the surface, one type of submerged plant, one type of fish, and one frog.
working: A is false — there are multiple types of floating plants, so they do not make up one population. B is true — fish and frogs are two populations of animals. C is false — there is only one community in a habitat (all populations living together), not separate communities for plants and animals.

subject: Science
question_text: Which of the following changes would make the alarm ring louder? A. Use a stiffer spring. B. Increase the number of coils of wire. C. Use a thicker iron bar to attach the hammer. (1) B only (2) C only (3) A and B only (4) B and C only
background_context: Electromagnetic alarm diagram with battery, coil, iron core, iron bar, hammer, bell, and spring.
working: A wrong — stiffer spring resists electromagnet pull. B correct — more coils = stronger electromagnet. C correct — thicker iron bar = more magnetic material attracted.
